mega.title: China Visa-Free Complete Guide
mega.desc: Travel to China without a visa! Comprehensive guide to China's 240-hour transit visa exemption policy, unilateral visa-free countries, and mutual visa exemption agreements.
section.title: About 240-Hour Transit Visa Exemption
section.desc: China has expanded its 144-hour and newly introduced 240-hour transit visa exemption policy for multiple countries. Eligible tourists and business travelers can enter through designated ports and stay in specified areas for up to 6-10 days without applying for a visa in advance.
hero.title: Visa Services
hero.desc: Master China visa information, easily handle visa applications, and start your China journey
transit.title: China 240-Hour Transit Visa Exemption
unilateral.title: Unilateral Visa-Free Countries
unilateral.desc: China offers visa-free entry for ordinary passport holders from the following countries for tourism, business, or family visits
mutual.title: Mutual Visa Exemption Countries
mutual.desc: Citizens of both countries can travel with ordinary passports without applying for a visa in advance for short-term visits
faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: When does the 240 hours start counting?
faq.a1: The 240 hours start from midnight of the day after entry. For example: if you enter on December 1st, the 10-day exemption period is from December 2nd 00:00 to December 11th 24:00.
faq.a2: Yes, you can freely travel within the provinces covered by the exemption policy within 10 days.
faq.q3: Can I enter mainland China from Hong Kong?
faq.a3: No, according to policy, foreign nationals entering from Hong Kong, Macau, or Taiwan are not eligible for the 240-hour transit exemption. You need to apply for the appropriate visa in advance.
faq.q4: Can I buy exit tickets after entering China?
faq.a4: No, you must have confirmed connecting tickets with fixed dates and seats in advance
faq.q5: Is it allowed to enter from Guangzhou and exit from Shanghai?
faq.a5: Yes, different ports of entry and exit are supported
faq.q6: How to apply for temporary entry permit?
faq.a6: Designated ports (such as Beijing Capital Airport T3) have dedicated counters. You need to fill out the Temporary Entry Foreigner Entry Card on site, submit passport + connecting ticket, and receive the permit after border inspection.
faq.q7: Does transit via Hong Kong/Macau apply?
faq.a7: Yes. For example: USA → Guangzhou → Hong Kong West Kowloon, you can apply for exemption with a high-speed rail ticket; Canada → Zhuhai → Macau, ferry tickets also apply.
faq.q8: Can connecting tickets include land transport?
faq.a8: Yes. Cross-border land-sea-air combined transport tickets such as Guangzhou → Hong Kong high-speed rail, Zhuhai → Macau ferry are all accepted.
faq.a9: Report to local public security immediately. Get a Passport Loss Report, apply for a stay permit at the exit-entry administration, and contact your embassy for travel document replacement.
faq.q10: How to choose between mutual visa exemption and transit exemption?
faq.a10: For citizens of mutual exemption countries like Singapore, use the 30-day exemption (more flexible); if only transit, choose 240-hour exemption (no visa fee).
faq.a11: Daily fine of 500 yuan, up to 10 days detention and deportation, and banned from transit exemption for 5 years.
faq.a12: Search for National Immigration Administration on WeChat, or call 12367 hotline (supports 8 languages including English, Japanese, Korean).
faq.a13: Including commercial photography, livestreaming for profit, temporary teaching, etc., may be considered illegal even without labor contracts.
faq.q14: Special arrangements at Beijing Capital Airport?
faq.a14: T3 terminal has a Transit Exemption Fast Track with English/Japanese/Korean multilingual staff, reducing processing time to within 15 minutes. Note: 22:00-6:00 period needs to use 24-hour emergency window.
faq.q15: How to connect at Shanghai Pudong Airport?
faq.a15: Air-Rail intermodal service available. With high-speed rail tickets, you can complete transit procedures directly at the satellite hall. Example: Paris → Shanghai → Hangzhou East Station.
faq.a16: Commercial photography requires work visa, but personal tourism photography is allowed. Drone aerial photography is prohibited in no-fly zones (500m around military facilities), with fines up to 20,000 yuan.
faq.a17: One free change allowed, must be completed before the 7th day of stay. If changing from D10 Hong Kong flight to D11, you need to register the new ticket at the entry port before D7.
faq.q18: Are cross-border high-speed rail tickets accepted?
faq.a18: International train tickets like China-Laos Railway (Kunming → Vientiane), China-Vietnam Railway (Nanning → Hanoi) are accepted. Both paper tickets and electronic ticket numbers need verification.
faq.q19: How to apply for pre-clearance on WeChat?
faq.a19: Submit passport photo page + e-ticket on the Immigration Bureau mini-program. AI generates Transit Pre-clearance Code in 10 minutes, reducing port processing time by 50%.
faq.a20: Avoid taking photos making V-signs in front of government buildings, photographing ethnic minority clothing without permission. Dress code applies at religious sites.
faq.q21: How to seek medical help for sudden illness?
faq.a21: With passport, you can register for emergency at Level 2+ hospitals. Recommend 53 designated foreign-friendly hospitals like Peking Union Medical College Hospital, Ruijin Hospital Shanghai.
faq.q22: How to extend stay due to sudden illness?
faq.a22: Provide hospital diagnosis certificate, apply to municipal public security exit-entry administration at least 3 working days before expiration. Maximum 30-day extension available.
type.tourism.desc: For tourists entering China, including group tours. Stay duration is typically 30, 60, or 90 days. Single or multiple entry available.
type.business.desc: For persons entering for commercial trade activities. First stay up to 90 days, extendable.
type.familyQ1.desc: Family members of Chinese citizens or permanent residents applying for long-term residence in China (over 180 days).
type.work.desc: Foreign experts entering for work; commercial performances; chief representatives of foreign company offices; offshore oil operations; volunteers (over 90 days); others with work permits issued by Chinese government authorities.
type.studyX1.desc: Long-term study (over 180 days). Requires admission notice and JW201/JW202 form.
type.studyX2.desc: Short-term study (not exceeding 180 days). For short courses or exchange programs.
type.transit.desc: Persons passing through China to a third country. Stay duration typically not exceeding 10 days.
type.crew.desc: International train crew; aircraft crew; ship crew and accompanying family members; international road transport drivers.
type.permanent.desc: Requires prior approval from Ministry of Public Security. For persons applying for permanent residence in China.
type.exchange.desc: Academic exchange; cultural exchange; religious exchange; NGO exchange; volunteers (not exceeding 90 days); foreign experts with Invitation Letter; geographical surveying.
type.journalistJ1.desc: Foreign journalists stationed in China. Requires notification letter from MFA Press Department.
type.journalistJ2.desc: Short-term interview journalists (stay not exceeding 180 days). Requires official media letter.
type.talent.desc: Provides streamlined entry and residence policies for urgently needed foreign high-level talent.
type.privateS1.desc: Long-term family visitors of foreigners staying in China (over 180 days).
type.privateS2.desc: Short-term family visits (not exceeding 180 days) or persons needing to stay for other private affairs.
doc.passport.desc: Passport valid for 6+ months with blank visa pages, original and copy
doc.formAndPhoto.desc: Completed PRC Visa Application Form, 1 recent white background photo (48mm×33mm)
doc.residence.desc: Non-nationality applicants need to provide valid residence or visa proof
doc.previous.desc: Former Chinese nationals or changed passport holders need to provide original passport or old visa copy

countries.americas: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Mexico, USA, Canada
countries.asia: Brunei, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, UAE, Qatar
seo.title: China Visa & Visa Exemption Policy
seo.description: Learn about China's latest 144/240-hour transit visa exemption policy, unilateral visa-free countries, port details and entry precautions.
seo.keywords: China visa, 144-hour exemption, 240-hour exemption, transit visa, China entry policy
sidebar.portDesc1: Eligible: Citizens of 54 countries (US, UK, Japan, etc.)
sidebar.portDesc2: Requirement: Valid only at 60 designated ports like Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou. Other ports not applicable.
sidebar.materialTitle: Document Preparation: Connecting Ticket + Valid Passport
sidebar.materialDesc2: Confirmed connecting ticket (to third country within 240 hours, cannot return to departure country)
sidebar.materialDesc3: Process: Submit documents to port customs upon entry, receive entry permit after approval.
sidebar.areaDesc1: Allowed: Tourism or intra-province travel within 24 provinces/municipalities like Beijing, Shanghai, Sichuan.
sidebar.ruleDesc1: Time limit: Maximum 240-hour stay (calculated from midnight after entry)
sidebar.ruleDesc2: Overstay/Out of area: Must apply to public security for exit-stay documents (only for emergencies or force majeure).
sidebar.ruleDesc3: Registration: Must register accommodation within 24 hours (hotel or police station).
sidebar.ruleDesc4: Only for short-term tourism/business activities. Work or study prohibited.
sidebar.disclaimer: Disclaimer: Information provided is for reference only. For specific policies, please refer to official releases from China's National Immigration Administration. Policies may change anytime, please confirm latest regulations before departure.
nav.title: Visa Services
visa.page.types.title: Visa Types
visa.page.fee.reciprocalDesc: Some countries have reciprocal visa agreements with China (e.g., USA, UK, Canada), which may result in higher fees than for non-reciprocal countries.
visa.page.fee.expedited: Expedited service: Usually an additional 300-500 RMB.
visa.page.fee.mailing: Mailing fee: Approx. 50-100 RMB (if mailing documents).
visa.page.fee.medical: Medical exam fee: Approx. 500-800 RMB (required for some visa types).
visa.page.fee.feeNote1: The above fees are for reference only; exact amounts are subject to the latest announcements from Chinese embassies, consulates, or visa centers.
visa.page.fee.feeNote2: It is recommended to check the specific fee standards for your country on the embassy website or consult a local visa agency in advance.
visa.page.photo.generalReqDesc: Submit two photos of the applicant taken within the last 6 months. The background must be white, without shadows on the face or background. Each photo must show a clear, full frontal view of the applicant's face, neck, and upper shoulders. Print on matte or glossy photo paper in color, without any modifications. If photos don't meet requirements, new ones will be requested.
visa.page.photo.photoDimensionsDesc: Dimensions: 33mm wide X 48mm high. Head width: 15-22mm, head height (from bottom of chin to top of head): 28-33mm. Space from top of head to top of photo: 3-5mm.
visa.page.photo.faceReqDesc: Facial expression must be neutral, eyes open, all facial features clearly visible. Glasses allowed only if lenses are uncolored and glare-, shadow-, or frame-free. Hearing aids/similar devices allowed.
visa.page.photo.headwearReqDesc: Hats or headwear are permitted only if worn for religious reasons and do not obscure any facial features.
visa.page.photo.digitalReqDesc: Dimensions: 354~472px high, 420~560px wide. Face width: 191~251px. Eyes: Approx. 256px from bottom edge. Top margin: 10~70px. Submit as JPEG image, size 40-120 KB.
visa.page.photo.digitalNote: Note: If submitting electronically, please bring two printed photos to your interview.
visa.page.card.step1Desc: Search for "Foreigner Arrival Card" and access the website. Links vary by city; please select the corresponding link for your port of entry.
visa.page.card.step2Desc: Click on "Foreigner Arrival Card" or "24/240-hour visa-free transit".
visa.page.card.step3Desc: Select language (English, Japanese, Korean, etc.), and fill in basic information such as name, gender, nationality, and document number.
visa.page.card.step4Desc: Fill in relevant information such as flight number, contact phone number, purpose of entry, and address.
visa.page.card.step5Desc: After completion, save the QR code to your phone for convenience during entry.
visa.page.card.step6Desc: Scan the QR code at the self-service machine to print the arrival card, submit it to immigration, complete entry procedures, and you can enter after fingerprint scanning!
visa.page.download.doc3Title: Application Form for Taiwan Residents Settling in Mainland

visa.countries.americas: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Mexico, USA, Canada
visa.countries.asia: Brunei, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, UAE, Qatar
visa.type.tourism.desc: For tourists entering China, including group tours. Stay is usually 30, 60, or 90 days, with single or multiple entry options.
visa.type.business.desc: Issued to persons entering for commercial and trade activities. Initial stay can be up to 90 days, extendable.
visa.type.familyQ1.desc: For family members of Chinese citizens or permanent residents applying for long-term stay (over 180 days) in China.
visa.type.familyQ2.desc: For short-term family visits (not exceeding 180 days) and relatives' short stays.
visa.type.work.desc: Foreign experts entering for work; commercial performances; chief representatives of foreign enterprises; offshore oil operations; volunteers (over 90 days); other personnel entering for work with work permits issued by relevant Chinese government departments.
visa.type.studyX1.desc: For long-term studies (over 180 days). School admission notice and JW201/JW202 forms are required.
visa.type.studyX2.desc: For short-term studies (not exceeding 180 days). Applicable to short courses and exchange programs.
visa.type.transit.desc: For personnel passing through China to a third country. Stay is usually not more than 10 days.
visa.type.crew.desc: International train crew; international aircraft crew; international ship crew and accompanying family members; international road transport drivers.
visa.type.permanent.desc: Prior approval from the Ministry of Public Security required. Applicable to those applying for permanent residence in China.
visa.type.exchange.desc: Academic exchange activities; cultural exchange activities; religious exchange activities; NGO exchange activities; volunteers (not exceeding 90 days); foreign experts with 'Invitation Letter for Foreign Experts to China'; geographic surveying activities.
visa.type.journalistJ1.desc: Foreign journalists permanently based in Chinese news agencies. Notification letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Press Department required.
visa.type.journalistJ2.desc: Short-term interview journalists (stay not exceeding 180 days). Official media seal required.
visa.type.talent.desc: Provides streamlined entry and stay policies for foreign high-level talents urgently needed by the country.
visa.type.privateS1.desc: Long-term visiting family members (over 180 days) of foreigners staying in China.
visa.type.privateS2.desc: Short-term visits (not exceeding 180 days) or other private affairs requiring stay in China.
visa.doc.L.title: Tourist (L) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.passport.desc: Passport valid for more than 6 months with blank visa pages, original and copy
visa.doc.formAndPhoto.desc: Completed《中华人民共和国签证申请表》, 1 recent white background photo (48mm×33mm)
visa.doc.residence.desc: Non-citizens must provide valid residence or visa documents from their country
visa.doc.previous.desc: Former Chinese citizens or those who changed passports must provide previous passport or old visa copy
visa.doc.L.itinerary.desc: Documentation showing travel plans in China (hotel bookings, flight tickets, etc.)
visa.doc.L.invitation.desc: Invitation letter and travel schedule prepared by Chinese travel agency or host
visa.doc.M.title: Business (M) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.M.invitation.desc: Invitation letter from Chinese business partner or inviting organization (including company name, visit purpose, itinerary)
visa.doc.M.business.desc: Company documents, or trade-related documents if no prior relationship (contracts, invoices, etc.)
visa.doc.Q1.title: Family (Q1) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.Q1.invitation.desc: 'Chinese Family Member Interview Invitation' or 'Invitation Letter for Children of Chinese Nationals Born Abroad'
visa.doc.Q1.relation.desc: Notarized relationship certificate translated to Chinese or English
visa.doc.Q1.residence.desc: Foreigner's Residence Permit in China or copy of valid passport of foreigner residing in China
visa.doc.Q2.title: Family (Q2) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.Q2.invitation.desc: Invitation letter from family member in China and copy of household registration
visa.doc.Z.title: Work (Z) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.Z.work.desc: 'People's Republic of China Foreigner Work Permit' or 'Foreign Expert Work Permit'
visa.doc.Z.health.desc: Foreigner Physical Examination Record or health certificate from government-approved medical institution
visa.doc.Z.invitation.desc: Invitation from government department or employing organization
visa.doc.X1.title: Study (X1) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.X1.admission.desc: JW201 or JW202 form issued by Chinese government, or school admission notice
visa.doc.X1.scholarship.desc: Proof of Chinese government scholarship recipient or scholarship notification from government
visa.doc.X1.health.desc: Foreigner Physical Examination Record or health certificate from approved institution
visa.doc.X2.title: Study (X2) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.X2.admission.desc: School admission notice or enrollment notification
visa.doc.G.title: Transit (G) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.G.ticket.desc: Confirmed ticket to third country (departure within 10 days of entry)
visa.doc.C.title: Crew (C) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.C.crew.desc: International transport crew certificate, seaman's book, or flight crew certificate
visa.doc.C.invitation.desc: Invitation or guarantee letter from transport operator
visa.doc.D.title: Permanent (D) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.D.confirm.desc: Foreigner's Permanent Residence Application Confirmation issued by Ministry of Public Security
visa.doc.D.criminal.desc: Criminal record certificate issued by government of country of residence
visa.doc.D.health.desc: Health certificate from government-approved medical institution
visa.doc.D.pass.desc: Valid travel document issued by Chinese government
visa.doc.F.title: Exchange (F) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.F.invitation.desc: Invitation from government department or inviting organization (may include JW201/JW202 form)
visa.doc.F.exchange.desc: Academic conference notice, cultural exchange activity plan, etc.
visa.doc.J1.title: Journalist (J1) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.J1.notification.desc: Notification from Ministry of Foreign Affairs Press Department
visa.doc.J1.media.desc: Assignment letter and copy of journalist ID from employer
visa.doc.J2.title: Journalist (J2) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.J2.notification.desc: Notification from Ministry of Foreign Affairs Press Department
visa.doc.J2.media.desc: Assignment letter and copy of journalist ID from employer
visa.doc.R.title: Talent (R) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.R.talent.desc: High-level talent certificate or foreign talent confirmation issued by government
visa.doc.R.invitation.desc: Invitation from employer or government department
visa.doc.S1.title: Private Affairs (S1) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.S1.invitation.desc: Invitation letter from host in China and proof of relationship
visa.doc.S1.relation.desc: Notarized relationship certificate or birth certificate translated to Chinese/English
visa.doc.S2.title: Private Affairs (S2) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.S2.invitation.desc: Invitation letter from host in China (including relevant supporting documents for private affairs)
visa.doc.L.title: Tourist (L) Visa Required Documents
visa.doc.passport.desc: Passport valid for more than 6 months with blank visa pages, original and copy
visa.doc.formAndPhoto.desc: Completed《中华人民共和国签证申请表》, 1 recent white background photo (48mm×33mm)
visa.doc.residence.desc: Non-citizens must provide valid residence or visa documents from their country
visa.doc.previous.desc: Former Chinese citizens or those who changed passports must provide previous passport or old visa copy
visa.doc.L.itinerary.desc: Documentation showing travel plans in China (hotel bookings, flight tickets, etc.)
visa.doc.L.invitation.desc: Invitation letter and travel schedule prepared by Chinese travel agency or host
visa.unilateral.title: Unilateral Visa-Free Countries
visa.unilateral.desc: Citizens of these countries can stay in China for up to 30 days without a visa for tourism, business, or visiting purposes.
visa.mutual.title: Mutual Visa Exemption Countries
visa.mutual.desc: Based on mutual visa exemption agreements, citizens of these countries can enjoy visa-free travel to China.
visa.mutual.AlbaniaDays: Maximum stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.AntiguaBarbudaDays: 30 days; cumulative stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.ArmeniaDays: Maximum stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.BelarusDays: 30 days, not exceeding 90 days per year
visa.mutual.BosniaHerzegovinaDays: Maximum stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.GeorgiaDays: 30 days, cumulative stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.KazakhstanDays: 30 days, maximum stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.SolomonIslandsDays: 30 days, cumulative stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.SamoaDays: 30 days, cumulative stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.ThailandDays: 30 days, maximum stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days
visa.mutual.UzbekistanDays: 30 days, cumulative stay of no more than 90 days per 180 days

visa.transit.ports.Beijing.Ports: Beijing Capital International Airport, Beijing Daxing International Airport
visa.transit.ports.Tianjin.Ports: Tianjin Binhai International Airport, Tianjin Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Hebei.Ports: Shijiazhuang Zhengding International Airport, Qinhuangdao Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Liaoning.Ports: Shenyang Taoxian International Airport, Dalian Zhoushuizi International Airport, Dalian Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Shanghai.Ports: Shanghai Hongqiao International Airport, Shanghai Pudong International Airport, Shanghai Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Jiangsu.Ports: Nanjing Lukou International Airport, Sunan Shuofang International Airport, Yangzhou Taizhou International Airport, Lianyungang Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Zhejiang.Ports: Hangzhou Xiaoshan International Airport, Ningbo Lishe International Airport, Wenzhou Longwan International Airport, Yiwu Airport, Wenzhou Port (Passenger), Zhoushan Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Anhui.Ports: Hefei Xingiao International Airport, Huangshan Tunxi International Airport
visa.transit.ports.Fujian.Ports: Fuzhou Changle International Airport, Xiamen Gaogi International Airport, Quanzhou Jinjiang International Airport, Wuyishan Airport, Xiamen Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Shandong.Ports: Jinan Yaoqiang International Airport, Qingdao Jiaodong International Airport, Yantai Penglai International Airport, Weihai Dashuibo International Airport, Qingdao Port (Passenger)
visa.transit.ports.Hunan.Ports: Changsha Huanghua International Airport, Zhangjiajie Hehua International Airport
